Title: Rhamnosyltransferases involved in the biosynthesis of flavone rutinosides in Chrysanthemum species

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in flower bud; higher in flower buds of Ci-HB2x than Ci-GD4x
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; also detected in transgenic hairy roots and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial/pharmaceutically active flavone rutinosides; diosmin is pharmaceutically active
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT79 family UDP-glycosyltransferase with PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to the 6-position of the glucose moiety of flavonoid 7-O-glucosides, forming 1,6 sugar-sugar bonds and corresponding rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant for flavone rutinoside formation; bottleneck/rate-limiting status not definitively established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavone rutinosides, especially linarin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening from RNA-seq; recombinant protein enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS product identification; substrate specificity and kinetics; subcellular localization; overexpression in hairy roots; stable overexpression in Chrysanthemum plants; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79A20 was the only screened candidate from transcriptome data that catalyzed formation of linarin, isorhoifolin, and diosmin in vitro. 2. The enzyme is a cytosolic, flower-bud-enriched 1,6-rhamnosyltransferase acting on flavonoid glucosides. 3. Overexpression increased linarin, isorhoifolin, and diosmin in hairy roots by 13-, 2-, and 5-fold, respectively, and increased flavone rutinosides in transgenic plants, showing major contribution to linarin biosynthesis in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi plants did not survive, but specific metabolic or growth penalty from overexpression was not clearly established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Same coding sequence present in Ci-GD4x and Ci-HB4x. Enzyme is substrate-promiscuous and also accepts flavanone and flavonol glucosides in vitro, but shows highest preference for acacetin-7-O-glucoside. Study concludes it mainly contributes to linarin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of linarin, isorhoifolin, and diosmin; suitable enzyme for synthetic biology production of pharmaceutically active diosmin/isorhoifolin and for enhancing flavone rutinosides in Chrysanthemum
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison across diploid and tetraploid cytotypes/accessions; greenhouse-grown plants

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT79 family UDP-glycosyltransferase with PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to flavonoid glucosides to form corresponding rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of flavone rutinosides in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology cloning; recombinant protein enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79A21 is one of four functional Chrysanthemum 1,6RhaTs. 2. It converts acacetin-, apigenin-, and diosmetin-7-O-glucosides into their rutinosides. 3. It shows similar catalytic efficiency to other functional Chrysanthemum 1,6RhaTs and prefers acacetin-7-O-glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate-promiscuous in vitro, also acts on flavanone and flavonol glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme resource for flavone rutinoside engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (previous transcriptome used for candidate discovery; accession not provided)

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT79 family UDP-glycosyltransferase with PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose onto flavonoid glucosides to form rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavone rutinoside formation in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology cloning; recombinant protein enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79A22 is a functional Chrysanthemum 1,6RhaT. 2. It catalyzes production of linarin, isorhoifolin, and diosmin from corresponding 7-O-glucosides. 3. It shares identical coding sequence between Ci-HB2x and Ci-AH2x and prefers acacetin-7-O-glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Associated with high linarin accumulation in diploid C. indicum accessions, though metabolite level differences also depend on expression and genetic background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme resource for linarin improvement

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGT79 family UDP-glycosyltransferase with PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to flavonoid glucosides, generating rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavone rutinoside formation in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology cloning; recombinant protein enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79A23 is a functional 1,6RhaT from C. nankingense. 2. It catalyzes conversion of flavone glucosides to linarin, isorhoifolin, and diosmin in vitro. 3. Its catalytic efficiency is similar to other functional Chrysanthemum 1,6RhaTs and it prefers acacetin-7-O-glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme source for flavonoid rutinoside engineering

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Chrysanthemum indicum; in vitro recombinant system
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavone glucosides including acacetin-7-O-glucoside; apigenin-7-O-glucoside; diosmetin-7-O-glucoside; UDP-rhamnose donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Corresponding rutinosides including linarin; isorhoifolin; diosmin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 1,6-rhamnosylation of flavonoid glucosides to form rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Demonstrated cross-genus catalytic capacity toward Chrysanthemum flavone glucosides in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis; recombinant protein assay; LC-MS/MS substrate conversion assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cm1,6RhaT from Citrus maxima can convert Chrysanthemum flavone glucosides into corresponding rutinosides in vitro. 2. This supports shared catalytic capability/common evolutionary origin between Citrus and Chrysanthemum 1,6RhaTs. 3. In Citrus it is known mainly for flavanone rutinoside formation, but in this paper it also accepted flavone glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included for biochemical comparison; not the main in planta gene of Chrysanthemum metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme tool for synthetic biology production of rutinosides

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Chrysanthemum indicum; in vitro recombinant system
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavone glucosides and flavanone glucosides; UDP-rhamnose donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Corresponding rutinosides including linarin; isorhoifolin; diosmin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes transfer of rhamnose to the glucose moiety of flavonoid glucosides to form rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts Chrysanthemum flavone glucosides to rutinosides in vitro and shows stronger preference for flavanone glucosides than flavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis; recombinant protein assay; substrate specificity comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1,6RhaT catalyzes conversion of Chrysanthemum flavone glucosides to corresponding rutinosides in vitro. 2. It prefers flavanone glucosides over flavone glucosides. 3. Comparison with Chrysanthemum enzymes supports common evolutionary origin but different substrate preference shaping metabolite diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mainly as comparative enzyme, not primary Chrysanthemum in planta target
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential catalyst for engineering flavanone/flavone rutinosides
